title: 有翼之暗
summary:
Woodside asks Inaba for advice regarding the potential location of the killer's hidden head. Inaba suggests the location is "Hell's Gate," a specific room within the mansion. Led by Jiban, the son of a neighbor, a large group—including Kaname, Mutsu, the narrator, Detective Horikawa, Kumagai, Kumozawa, Wakamatsu, Wakuzaki, Tsubaki, Detective Toriyama, Sugayan, and Kanou—navigates a dark, narrow, and unsettling passage to reach the room.

The group arrives at a thick, ivory-colored marble door decorated with exquisite carvings depicting the torments of hell, including naked bodies and an inverted Eastern Orthodox cross. While Kumozawa observes the artistry and Wakamatsu notes that the structure does not appear to have been originally intended as a door, Wakuzaki uses an oil lamp to inspect the upper carvings, discovering small, soybean-sized holes recessed into the design. Detective Toriyama finds the door locked, but Sugayan is able to unlock it with a key.

The narrative then shifts to a prologue set the previous day. The narrator, Yuki Hikari, visits an office where Mukai and Kumazawa are present. Kumazawa has opened two envelopes addressed to Murakami Yusuke: one white and one light brown. The brown envelope is covered in a white powder used for fingerprint extraction. The letters contain no sender information. It is noted that Murakami's father operates the Murakami Detective Agency, an elite and reputable firm serving the Kyoto and Kansai regions.
